main_event: Approximately 20 Airmen and 2 Civilians at MacDill AFB, Florida observed 1-3 elongated objects heading north and gaining altitude for approximately 20 minutes on 13 August 1952; one object made two abrupt 90-degree turns and appeared to move in excess of 600 MPH before resuming northerly course.
sighted_object: elongated, indefinite in shape, of unidentifiable color; possibly 1 to 3 objects; no visible aerodynamic features, no trail or exhaust, no visible propulsion system; two abrupt 90-degree turns (west then east); speed in excess of 600 MPH after turn; appeared to emanate white light from upper surface (possibly reflected sunlight); one Airman described egg-shaped, others said shapeless like airborne sheet of paper
witness_description: Approximately 20 Airmen and 2 Civilians; observers located on street side of MacDill Base Hangar; mostly young inexperienced Airmen but three NCOs and two Civilians appeared sincere, experienced, and completely reliable; report prepared by Capt. A.M.J. Grant, Hq 6th Air Division Intelligence Officer, by personal interrogation
interesting_points: No ATIC conclusion checkbox checked on record card — case appears unresolved
One object veered abruptly west at 600+ MPH, hovered ~5 minutes, then returned east at same speed, then resumed northward course
Two other objects (if they existed) continued north without maneuvering and disappeared
Radar at Base Operations was shut down for maintenance at time of sighting; no radar contact established
At least a dozen observers saw only one object; the majority disagreed on whether there were 1, 2, or 3 objects
A B-50 flew into observers' line of vision during sighting; object appeared to be at greater range than the B-50 (5+ miles away at ~5,000 feet)
Preparing officer considered report reliable: 'a sufficient number of these people are experienced and sincere'
CSV flag B:witnesses_22_not_in_text was validator false positive; '20 Airmen and two Civilians' = 22 is in the text
